policy.new_feature: Every new feature must add or update one feature scenario, one observable acceptance check, and one negative or boundary-oriented check unless explicitly recorded as a planned gap.
policy.literal_token_rule: The selected check item count must be at least 2.0 times the implementation-code token count. Check items include semantic checks plus C0 statement items, C1 branch outcome items, and C2 condition outcome items.
policy.random: Randomized tests must be seed-fixed by default and record the seed and case fingerprint.
policy.artifacts: Long-running, device, benchmark, monkey, variance, and abnormal-event runs must emit machine-readable artifacts tied to git commit, build flavor, command, seed when applicable, and summary.
policy.advanced_methods: Mutation, property-based, differential, stateful model, concurrency/race, crash/recovery, fault injection, golden corpus, security/adversarial, performance regression, and refactor-resilience methods must be represented in the feature scenario ledger. Unimplemented methods must be explicit planned gaps rather than invisible omissions.
